old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-hgmb","title":"BLE transport: Uses DLE length instead of ATT MTU for GATT notification chunking","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/ble_ipsp_transport.c
Lines: 124, 519

The code at line 124 reads `info.le.data_len->tx_max_len` (LE Data Length Extension PDU max) but uses it as if it were the ATT MTU:

```c
transport_state.mtu = info.le.data_len->tx_max_len;
```

Then at line 519, this value is used for chunking GATT notifications:

```c
uint16_t chunk_size = transport_state.mtu > 3 ? transport_state.mtu - 3 : 20;
```

The comment says 'ATT MTU includes 3-byte ATT header' but the value used is NOT ATT MTU - it is the Link Layer Data Length Extension maximum PDU size. These are different:
- DLE tx_max_len: Link layer PDU payload (27-251 bytes, link layer optimization)
- ATT MTU: Application layer max for single ATT operation (23-517 bytes)

GATT notification max payload is ATT MTU - 3 (for opcode + handle). Using DLE length instead could:
1. Result in oversized notifications being rejected by BLE stack
2. Result in suboptimal chunking

Fix: Use `bt_gatt_get_mtu(conn)` to get the negotiated ATT MTU, not DLE length.

Note: This is separate from project-LICHEN-tr8q which covers the NULL dereference issue on data_len.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:30:58.234449Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T17:30:58.234494Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-hgmb","title":"BLE transport: Uses DLE length instead of ATT MTU for GATT notification chunking","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/ble_ipsp_transport.c
Lines: 124, 519

The code at line 124 reads `info.le.data_len->tx_max_len` (LE Data Length Extension PDU max) but uses it as if it were the ATT MTU:

```c
transport_state.mtu = info.le.data_len->tx_max_len;
```

Then at line 519, this value is used for chunking GATT notifications:

```c
uint16_t chunk_size = transport_state.mtu > 3 ? transport_state.mtu - 3 : 20;
```

The comment says 'ATT MTU includes 3-byte ATT header' but the value used is NOT ATT MTU - it is the Link Layer Data Length Extension maximum PDU size. These are different:
- DLE tx_max_len: Link layer PDU payload (27-251 bytes, link layer optimization)
- ATT MTU: Application layer max for single ATT operation (23-517 bytes)

GATT notification max payload is ATT MTU - 3 (for opcode + handle). Using DLE length instead could:
1. Result in oversized notifications being rejected by BLE stack
2. Result in suboptimal chunking

Fix: Use `bt_gatt_get_mtu(conn)` to get the negotiated ATT MTU, not DLE length.

Note: This is separate from project-LICHEN-tr8q which covers the NULL dereference issue on data_len.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T17:30:58.234449Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T20:07:44.900228203Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T20:07:44.900228203Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T20:12:44.900228203Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T20:07:44.900228203Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Bug fixed in current implementation: connected_cb logs bt_gatt_get_mtu(conn) at line 128; lichen_ble_slip_send uses uint16_t att_mtu = bt_gatt_get_mtu(transport_state.conn); chunk_size = att_mtu \u003e 3 ? att_mtu-3 : 20 at lines 538-539. Removed unsafe info.le.data_len dereference. Commit hash: 7962be0b9 (part of broader codereview fixes). No further changes needed."}

Fixed by replacing DLE tx_max_len with bt_gatt_get_mtu(conn) in ble_ipsp_transport.c. Verified in current codebase (commit 7962be0b9). Related NULL deref bead project-LICHEN-tr8q also resolved by same change.
